rule.name: local/max-total-depth
rule.flags: Indentation deeper than configured maximum (default 3 levels)
rule.fix: Extract deeply indented blocks into separate functions at module scope

rule.name: local/no-nested-function
rule.flags: Parameterized function inside another parameterized function. Two permitted nesting shapes: (1) Nullary PA — inner has zero params and is not parameterized inside parameterized, (2) IIFE thunk — (() => (node) => work)() collapses by the 1 -> T isomorphism
rule.fix: Three solutions: (1) Extract inner function to module scope, pass free variables as explicit args (lambda lifting). (2) Nullary PA: (fn, ...params) => () => fn(...params). (3) IIFE thunk: (() => (node) => work)()

rule.name: local/restrict-return-count
rule.flags: More than 1 return statement per function, or any non-final return (early return)
rule.fix: Ternary for 2-3 branches. Nullish coalescing chain (tryA(x) ?? tryB(x) ?? fallback) for T|undefined dispatch. Record dispatch for 4+ branches

rule.name: local/enforce-record-type
rule.flags: Type alias body is an object literal type (TSTypeLiteral)
rule.fix: Rewrite { key: T } as Record<K, V> or restructure

rule.name: local/max-type-nesting
rule.flags: More than 1 nested type construct (TSTypeLiteral or TSTupleType) in a type alias
rule.fix: Extract inner type literals and tuples into named type aliases

rule.name: local/no-duplicate-type-structure
rule.flags: Two or more type aliases with identical canonical structural representation
rule.fix: Remove duplicate, reuse the existing type alias. If semantically distinct, differentiate the structure

rule.name: local/no-single-field-type
rule.flags: Type alias with exactly one member in a type literal body (except call signatures)
rule.fix: Inline the single field type directly or combine with other fields

rule.name: local/require-extracted-function-type
rule.flags: Inline type annotations that are not keywords or named references (TSTypeReference)
rule.fix: Extract to a named type alias: type TFoo = string | number; const x: TFoo = ...

rule.name: local/require-parametric-record
rule.flags: Record or mapped type without dependent product structure (value type does not reference key type parameter)
rule.fix: Add type parameter: type TFoo<T extends X> = Record<T, TBar<T>>. For mapped types: {[K in T]: F<K>}. Or replace with tuple if no parametric relationship exists

rule.name: local/valid-generics
rule.flags: Degenerate generic (body is just the type parameter: type TId<T> = T) or homogeneous generic (all params passed straight through: type TFoo<A,B> = TBar<A,B>)
rule.fix: Degenerate: remove type parameter, use inner type directly. Homogeneous: remove generic params (make simple alias) or add transformation logic

rule.name: local/require-rest-params-tuple
rule.flags: Type-level function signature with positional parameters instead of rest-params tuple
rule.fix: Wrap parameters in a named tuple type and spread: type TArgs = [a: A, b: B]; type TFn = (...args: TArgs) => R

rule.name: local/finite-domain-return-widening
rule.flags: Function from finite domain returns bare 'string' — prefer a literal union return type
rule.fix: Replace bare string return with a literal union that maps each input variant to a specific output. The function becomes a lookup table

rule.name: local/type-distance
rule.flags: Two type aliases with structural similarity >= 0.75 (Jaccard similarity on keys and values)
rule.fix: Merge the two types into one if they represent the same concept, or add/remove fields to make the structural difference explicit

rule.name: local/cardinality-isomorphic-families
rule.flags: Two generic types with the same constraint that have identical cardinality profiles (union members, record fields, tuple elements)
rule.fix: Unify the two generic types into a single parameterized type if they agree pointwise over their shared domain

rule.name: local/transport-graph
rule.flags: Discriminated type with no edges in the transport graph — not used as domain or codomain of any function signature
rule.fix: Define a function type that uses the isolated type as domain or codomain: type TFn = (x: TIsolated) => TTarget

rule.name: local/fiber-coherence
rule.flags: Two types connected by an isomorphic edge in the transport graph that have different cardinalities
rule.fix: Either adjust the types to have matching cardinalities (same number of variants/fields/elements) or reclassify the edge as a section

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment
rule.flags: Usage of @ts-ignore, @ts-expect-error, or @ts-nocheck comments
rule.fix: Remove the directive. Fix the underlying type error instead

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/ban-tslint-comment
rule.flags: Legacy TSLint directive comments (tslint:disable etc)
rule.fix: Remove the stale directive comment

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/consistent-generic-constructors
rule.flags: Generic type args on constructor call instead of type annotation
rule.fix: Move generics to the variable declaration: const x: Array<T> = new Array()

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/prefer-function-type
rule.flags: Type literal with only a single call signature
rule.fix: Simplify { (): T } to () => T

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/array-type
rule.flags: T[] array syntax
rule.fix: Use Array<T> syntax. Tuples are still allowed

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/consistent-type-assertions
rule.flags: Any type assertion (as X or <X> syntax)
rule.fix: Remove assertion. Restructure code so TypeScript infers the correct type. Use switch dispatch or try-dispatch pattern

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-type-assertion
rule.flags: Type assertions that narrow to a more specific type
rule.fix: Use type narrowing via discriminated unions or switch

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/consistent-type-definitions
rule.flags: Interface declarations (interface keyword)
rule.fix: Convert to type alias: interface IFoo becomes type TFoo

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports
rule.flags: Non-type imports of type-only symbols
rule.fix: Use import type { }

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/consistent-type-exports
rule.flags: Type exports without export type keyword
rule.fix: Use export type { }

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type
rule.flags: Function without explicit return type annotation
rule.fix: Add : TReturnType. Create the type alias first if needed

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/typedef
rule.flags: Variable declaration without type annotation
rule.fix: Add explicit type: const x: T = ...

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/no-deprecated
rule.flags: Use of deprecated APIs
rule.fix: Replace with the current API equivalent

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/no-duplicate-type-constituents
rule.flags: Redundant members in unions (A | A) or intersections (A & A)
rule.fix: Remove the duplicate constituent

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
rule.flags: Usage of any type (except rest args)
rule.fix: Replace with a specific type or unknown

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/method-signature-style
rule.flags: Method signature syntax foo(x: A): B in type literals
rule.fix: Use property style: foo: (x: A) => B

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition
rule.flags: Conditions that are always truthy or always falsy
rule.fix: Remove condition. Use try-dispatch for T | undefined lookups

rule.name: @typescript-eslint/no-confusing-void-expression
rule.flags: Void expressions in non-statement position
rule.fix: Use statement form. Arrow shorthand is exempt

rule.name: eslint-comments/no-use
rule.flags: Any eslint disable comment (no exceptions)
rule.fix: Fix the underlying issue structurally. No suppression allowed

rule.name: functional/no-let
rule.flags: Any let declaration
rule.fix: Use const. For dispatch: try/nullish-coalescing chain (tryA(x) ?? tryB(x) ?? fallback)

rule.name: arrow-body-style
rule.flags: Block body when expression body suffices
rule.fix: Remove braces and return keyword: => expr

rule.name: func-style
rule.flags: Function declarations (function keyword)
rule.fix: Convert to const arrow: const f: T = (...) => ...

rule.name: prefer-arrow-callback
rule.flags: Function expression as callback
rule.fix: Replace with arrow function

rule.name: indent
rule.flags: Non-4-space indentation
rule.fix: Restructure code to reduce indentation. Extract into smaller functions at module scope

rule.name: max-len
rule.flags: Lines exceeding 80 characters
rule.fix: Break into multiple lines. Use string concatenation for long strings. Extract sub-expressions

rule.name: max-lines-per-function
rule.flags: Functions exceeding 40 lines (including blanks and comments)
rule.fix: Extract helper functions at module scope

rule.name: complexity
rule.flags: Cyclomatic complexity exceeding 5
rule.fix: Extract branches into functions. Use try-dispatch pattern

rule.name: no-warning-comments
rule.flags: Comments containing prettier-ignore
rule.fix: Remove comment. Restructure code to work with prettier

rule.name: no-restricted-syntax
rule.flags: Type predicates (is keyword in return type)
rule.fix: Use discriminated unions or explicit type narrowing

rule.name: id-length
rule.flags: Identifiers shorter than 3 characters
rule.fix: Use descriptive names. Exceptions: id, i, j, k, x, y, z, _, fs, db, ui, el, e

rule_interactions.interaction: indent --fix + ternary chains → cascading max-total-depth violations. Prettier reformats ternaries; --fix re-indents; depth exceeds 3. Solution: use try-dispatch for 4+ branches.
restrict-return-count + if-chains → multiple returns. Solution: ternary (2-3 branches) or try-dispatch (4+).
consistent-type-assertions:never + Record dispatch → cannot cast. Solution: switch dispatch with type narrowing in each case.
functional/no-let + switch dispatch → tension. Known: switch+let is the only pattern satisfying return-count + depth + assertions simultaneously. Accepted trade-off.
Record access + no-unnecessary-condition → ?? flagged. Record<K,V>[k] returns V (never undefined), so ?? is unnecessary. Solution: use try-dispatch for lookups needing T|undefined.
prettier ternary formatting + indent → permanent incompatibility on deep ternaries. No prettier config fixes this. Solution: max 3 ternary branches.
no-nested-function + ESLint handler pattern → need PA boundary. Solution: IIFE (() => (node) => work)() or .bind(null, context).

canonical_patterns.pattern: For T|undefined returns: tryX(node) ?? tryY(node) ?? fallback. Each tryX returns T|undefined. Satisfies: single return, no let, no depth issues.
For mandatory returns: let result: T; switch(x) { case A: result = f(x); break; } return result; Satisfies: single return, depth ≤3, type narrowing in cases. Violates: no-let (accepted trade-off).
IIFE: (() => (node: T) => work)() Or .bind(): handler.bind(null, context) Both valid PA boundaries for no-nested-function.
Use "a" + "b" over template literals for long strings. Template literals cannot be broken across lines under max-len: 80.
